pages.title: Factions and Offices
pages.text: Two separate systems sitting on top of vanilla.$(br2)$(bold)Faction.$(/) Regional team — Desert, Island, or Knight. Faction equals one FTB Teams party; the party owner is the leader.$(br2)$(bold)Office.$(/) World-government seat — Chancellor, Treasurer, or Judge. Each capped at one holder.

pages.title: Regional factions (3)
pages.text: $(li)$(l:factions/desert)Desert$(/l) — nomad and centurion plate.$(li)$(l:factions/island)Island$(/l) — ronin and wanderer mystic.$(li)$(l:factions/knight)Knight$(/l) — old, silver, dragonslayer, spark, fog-guard.$(br)Each faction's regalia is leader-craft-only. No trader sells it; the recipe is the only source.

pages.title: World-government offices (3)
pages.text: $(li)$(l:factions/chancellor)Chancellor$(/l) — hero regalia and the Crown.$(li)$(l:factions/treasurer)Treasurer$(/l) — gilded-hunt set, sole minter of Numismatics currency.$(li)$(l:factions/judge)Judge$(/l) — crucible-knight set.$(br)You hold an office independently of your faction party — they don't conflict.

pages.title: Claiming a faction
pages.text: Faction membership is bound to an FTB Teams party. The party $(bold)owner$(/) runs:$(br)$(li)$(bold)/cabafaction claim <desert|island|knight>$(/) — bind your party to a faction.$(li)$(bold)/cabafaction release$(/) — release the binding.$(li)$(bold)/cabafaction info$(/) — show your current faction. Any party member can run this.$(br)One faction per party; one party per faction. The recipes appear in your grid as soon as the binding takes.

pages.title: How crafting is gated
pages.text: All faction and office regalia live on a hidden civic origin every player holds. The recipes light up only while your faction or office is set — release the faction or lose the office and the recipes vanish from your grid.
